angles: Platform vs Agent vs Hybrid: Copilot is a platform ecosystem, Claude Code is a pure terminal agent, Amp sits in between with CLI + IDE support
The 'Use Both' Strategy: Most expert developers run Copilot Pro ($10/mo) for inline completions + Claude Code Max ($100/mo) for complex autonomous tasks — the real question is where Amp fits
Sourcegraph's code graph advantage: Amp leverages Sourcegraph's legacy code search for superior cross-repo context understanding unlike Claude Code's single-repo focus
Three-mode AI strategy: Amp's smart/rush/deep modes offer cost optimization by matching model complexity to task difficulty — a feature neither Copilot nor Claude Code offers natively
Benchmark vs real-world gap: Claude Code dominates SWE-bench (87.6%) but enterprise teams report Copilot as the most trusted for daily workflows
The free tier equation: Amp's ad-supported free tier (now paused), Claude Code's $20 Pro plan, and Copilot's $0 free tier create different entry points for adoption
Team collaboration features: Amp's thread sharing and leaderboards vs Claude Code's solo-focused design vs Copilot's enterprise GitHub integration
Security and compliance: Amp SOC 2 Type II + Copilot enterprise compliance vs Claude Code's trust-based model for Fortune 500 adoption
